text: User Profiles have dedicated data mappings. First, ensure you are on the Map Phase.

onEnter: const hasProfile = SC.kernel.state.nodes.some(n => n.type === 'profile'); if(!hasProfile) { const n = SC.kernel.addNode({ title: 'New Profile', type: 'profile' }); SC.kernel.selectNode(n.id); SC.kernel.bridge.fetchTemplateData('tpl_user_profile').then(data => { SC.kernel.applyTemplateToNode(n.id, data); SC.render(); }); } else { const p = SC.kernel.state.nodes.find(n => n.type === 'profile'); SC.kernel.selectNode(p.id); SC.render(); }
text: User Profiles are a specialized node type. I've imported a template and selected it for us. Let's inspect it.
The inspector panel dynamically adapts its layout depending on the node type. User nodes get avatar and credential editors.
Let's close the inspector to finish up.
